evidence: README.md defines explicit governance for contract classes (runtime integration vs UI primitives), required markers, and staged compatibility migration policy.
src/integrations/supabase/client.ts implements a minimal public facade with @publicContract annotation and a narrow export surface (getSupabaseClient + initializeSupabaseClientRuntime).
src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts exposes one stable runtime error facade with a focused options contract and clear ownership comment.
src/domains/generation/types/generationViewRow.ts documents a three-tier contract decomposition (persisted + derived + optimistic) and reflects it directly in exported type composition.
src/shared/components/ui/cn.ts and src/shared/components/ui/button.tsx carry @uiContract markers and present stable UI primitive entrypoints consistent with README governance scope.
high_level_elegance.unreported_risk: Governance relies heavily on comments/README alignment; if check coverage in contract enforcement drifts, documentation can remain clean while runtime contract boundaries erode.

evidence: Task payload builders duplicate overlapping semantic fields across multiple envelopes (e.g., prompt/model/parent lineage in both top-level payload data and contracts) in src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts and src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts.

Consumers must reconcile several fallback locations for the same value (task_view_contract, individual_segment_params, orchestrator_details, full_orchestrator_payload, top-level fields) in src/shared/lib/taskParamsUtils.ts.
Large parameter bags with many optional fields are propagated through task builders, then selectively copied into per-mode payloads instead of being normalized once at a single boundary.

Two near-duplicate result contracts are maintained across app and edge boundaries (`src/shared/lib/operationResult.ts` and `supabase/functions/_shared/edgeOperation.ts`) with similar fields but different constructors/call shapes, forcing adapters instead of a single seam contract.

Error propagation is inconsistent in generation-task lookup: `resolveGenerationProjectScope` returns status objects for query failure while `getPrimaryTaskMappingsForGenerations` throws on query failure, and callers (`src/shared/lib/generationTaskCache.ts`) must add special try/catch handling.

JSON request parsing exposes overlapping APIs (`parseJsonBodyLoose`, `parseJsonBody`, `parseJsonBodyStrict`) with different failure channels (status flags, result union, or response object), increasing handler-specific glue code in edge functions.

`handleStorageOperations` returns outer success even when thumbnail work fails by embedding nested `thumbnailOutcome` result inside the success payload, creating a mixed partial-failure contract that downstream callers must remember to inspect manually.

useSegmentSlotMode.ts mixes reducer state, URL deep-linking effects, thumbnail derivation, structure-video overlap resolution, and segment slot assembly in one hook, creating dense local control flow.
ShotImageManagerContainer.tsx performs broad orchestration of many managers, conditional segment fetching logic, and local data-shape adaptation in a single function body.
joinClips.ts and payloadBuilder.ts contain large field-by-field object construction sections with repeated conditional spreads and manual copy logic, which is verbose and easy to desynchronize.

Orchestrator hooks have accumulated multiple independent responsibilities: `useTimelineOrchestrator` handles drag/drop, selection, zoom, endpoint drag, video-browser insertion, pending-state bookkeeping, and returns a deeply nested interaction/data model from one hook.

`useImageEditOrchestrator` composes four edit-mode subsystems and also assembles a very large context object, including placeholder state (`isFlippedHorizontally = false`, `isSaving = false`) that indicates interface expansion beyond currently-owned behavior.

Shot editor control surfaces are excessively wide (`UseGenerationControllerParams`, `UseEditingControllerParams`, and `ShotEditorLayoutProps`) and mostly forwarded through layers, creating high coupling and prop-drilling pressure.

Edge-function architecture is inconsistent within the same subsystem: `claim-next-task` uses shared runtime/request primitives, while `calculate-task-cost` and `generate-upload-url` reimplement method/body/auth/env/response scaffolding locally.

Dynamic payload surfaces are widely represented as Record<string, unknown> and cast back into concrete shapes (e.g., src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts:345,368,464 and src/shared/lib/tasks/imageGeneration.ts:327,348,426).

Parser-heavy modules rely on permissive casting and null/fallback behavior instead of explicit schema contracts (e.g., src/shared/lib/taskParamsUtils.ts:11,24-33,63 and src/shared/lib/generationTransformers.ts:335,456).

UI and runtime glue code still accepts unknown state and narrows ad hoc (e.g., src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useSegmentSlotMode.ts:114-126 and src/shared/components/ImageGenerationDetails.tsx:12-14,56).

Very large multi-responsibility files increase cognitive load and search cost: src/shared/lib/taskParamsUtils.ts (850 lines), src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx (766 lines), src/tools/join-clips/pages/JoinClipsPage.tsx (605 lines).

Single modules combine domain orchestration + data shaping + UI-state concerns (e.g., src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts:340-538 builds contracts, payloads, mode routing; src/shared/realtime/DataFreshnessManager.ts centralizes state, policies, diagnostics, UI toast side effects).

Cross-layer flow is hard to trace because canonical transformations are split across overlapping utilities (src/shared/lib/taskParamsUtils.ts and src/shared/lib/generationTransformers.ts both act as normalization hubs).

Generic identifiers dominate critical paths (`params`, `data`, `base`, `payload`, `details`), reducing semantic precision in orchestrator and parsing code (e.g., src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts:341-469; src/shared/lib/taskParamsUtils.ts:63-123).

Parallel primitives use divergent vocabulary for similar concepts (`OperationFailurePolicy`/`operationFailure` vs `EdgeFailurePolicy`/`edgeFailure`), making API discovery less predictable (src/shared/lib/operationResult.ts:1-61, supabase/functions/_shared/edgeOperation.ts:1-53).

Event names mix styles and specificity levels in a single map (`openSettings`, `mobilePaneOpen`, `timeline:pending-add`, `realtime:auth-heal`), which increases naming entropy over time (src/shared/lib/typedEvents.ts:8-45).

findings.summary: Generation domain row contracts embed UI optimistic state, blurring ownership boundaries across domain models.
evidence: `GenerationOptimisticOverlayFields` defines `isOptimistic` as UI/runtime-only but is merged into exported `GenerationRow`.
`ShotViewModel.images?: GenerationRow[]` imports that mixed contract, spreading UI concerns into shot-level domain typing.
findings.suggestion: Split canonical/domain row types from UI overlay/view-state types (for example `GenerationEntity` vs `GenerationUIState`) and only compose them at UI selector/view-model boundaries.

findings.summary: Overlay visibility and interaction policy is implemented through multiple parallel patterns rather than one clear architecture seam.
evidence: `ModalContainer` centralizes modal behavior via `useModal`, `preventOutsideClose`, and shared footer helpers.
`InfoTooltip` and `MediaTypeFilter` independently normalize optional controlled state through `useOverlayVisibilityState`.

`CreativePartnerPane` uses `useManagedTooltipBindings` directly with externally managed open/disabled state, while modal consumers (`InstallInstructionsModal`, `DescriptionModal`) rely on `ModalContainer`.
findings.suggestion: Define a single overlay ownership model (for example standardized controlled/uncontrolled contract + shared policy hook) and apply it consistently across tooltip/select/modal wrappers.

findings.summary: Task metadata is represented in multiple parallel payload layers, forcing fallback-driven parsing.
evidence: joinClips payload writes both contracts and overlapping orchestration details/top-level lineage fields (e.g., prompt/model/parent_generation_id).
travelBetweenImages payload similarly emits orchestration_contract + task_view_contract + many legacy/top-level fields.
taskParamsUtils derives values via cascading reads across task_view_contract, segment params, orchestrator_details, full_orchestrator_payload, and top-level keys.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a single versioned canonical task envelope (authoritative contract object) and constrain readers to that source first; keep legacy fields only in a one-way compatibility adapter that is isolated from display/runtime parsing.

findings.summary: Lightbox collaborators exchange broad remapped state objects instead of stable seam contracts.
evidence: useVideoLightboxRenderModel builds multiple derived slices and remaps many fields into new objects before passing to context/layout.
LightboxLayout reconstructs a model from context then projects it again into narrower model fragments (Pick types and per-component model wrappers).
ImageLightbox has similar multi-layer environment/shared-state assembly, indicating repeated integration pattern rather than localized complexity.
findings.suggestion: Define a smaller explicit `LightboxRenderState` boundary (media, variants, navigation, panel) and have one assembler per mode produce that shape directly; let layout/components consume this contract without re-projection layers.

findings.summary: Core hooks combine unrelated local concerns, producing dense control flow and brittle edits.
evidence: useSegmentSlotMode includes UI reducer/actions, router-state parsing, deep-link side effects, structure overlap transforms, and media thumbnail traversal logic.
useShotImageManagerContainerState orchestrates many manager hooks plus segment fetch/selection/reconciliation logic in one function.
Both files interleave data shaping and side effects, making local reasoning and testing difficult at function scope.
findings.suggestion: Split each hook into: pure selectors/transformers, navigation/deep-link effects, and domain-specific orchestration hooks; keep top-level hooks as thin composition layers with minimal branching.

findings.summary: Large manual object assembly blocks create repetitive, error-prone local implementation detail.
evidence: joinClips per-join overrides copy many optional fields one-by-one into `joinSettings`.
travel payload base constructs a long mixed object with many conditional spreads and default applications.
individualTravelSegment defines extensive legacy/runtime param lists and broad state interfaces, indicating similar local assembly pressure.
findings.suggestion: Introduce reusable field-mapper helpers (schema-driven pick/rename/default application) for task payload assembly so each task mode declares mappings declaratively instead of repeating imperative copy logic.

findings.summary: App and edge layers maintain parallel result-envelope APIs with overlapping semantics.
evidence: `OperationResult` and `EdgeOperationResult` both encode `{ ok, policy, recoverable, ... }` but are defined independently with separate constructors (`operationFailure` vs `edgeFailure`).

Consumers repeatedly convert result objects back into thrown errors (`toOperationResultError` in `metadata.ts` and `useLineageChain.ts`), indicating seam friction rather than one stable integration contract.
findings.suggestion: Standardize on one cross-layer result envelope shape and helper naming, then migrate callers to that shared contract (or introduce a thin adapter module and make all boundary code use it consistently).

findings.summary: Generation-task repository mixes status-return and thrown-error failure modes for similar lookups.
evidence: `resolveGenerationProjectScope` returns `{ status: 'query_failed', queryError }` on DB failure, but `getPrimaryTaskMappingsForGenerations` throws `Error` on DB failure.

`preloadGenerationTaskMappings` must wrap `getPrimaryTaskMappingsForGenerations` in try/catch and skip the batch, while non-error misses are represented by status strings (`missing_generation`, `scope_mismatch`).
findings.suggestion: Unify repository failure behavior: either return typed status for all failure modes or throw for all hard failures, then update cache preload callers to follow one predictable branch model.

findings.summary: Edge request JSON parsing is exposed through overlapping APIs with different handoff contracts.
evidence: `parseJsonBodyLoose` returns `{ body, parseStatus, parseErrorCode }`, `parseJsonBody` returns `EdgeOperationResult<Record<string, unknown>>`, and `parseJsonBodyStrict` returns `{ body?; errorResponse? }`.
These three variants encode similar parsing outcomes through incompatible output shapes, so handlers must pick and adapt one-off patterns rather than consume a single boundary API.
findings.suggestion: Collapse to one primary parser contract (e.g., `EdgeOperationResult`) plus a single strict wrapper utility for response conversion, and deprecate the extra surface.

findings.summary: Storage API returns success while embedding thumbnail failure as nested result, obscuring boundary behavior.
evidence: `handleStorageOperations` returns `edgeSuccess({ ..., thumbnailOutcome })` even when thumbnail processing returns `edgeFailure('degrade', ...)`.
This creates a two-level outcome contract (outer `ok` plus inner `thumbnailOutcome.ok`) that is easy to mishandle at integration boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Promote partial-thumbnail failure to a first-class top-level response field/schema (e.g., explicit `warnings` array) or return a single normalized result that cannot hide nested failures.

findings.summary: Hook module also owns generic drop/upload utilities, blurring package responsibility.
evidence: useFileDragTracking.ts exports the hook plus createSingleFileDropHandler and preventDefaultDragOver, which are utility-style primitives rather than hook lifecycle/state behavior.
dragDrop.ts and dragDropCore.ts already hold drag/drop primitives, creating a split ownership model where similar concerns are divided across hooks and lib packages.
findings.suggestion: Keep src/shared/hooks/useFileDragTracking.ts hook-only, and move createSingleFileDropHandler/preventDefaultDragOver into a dedicated lib module (for example src/shared/lib/dragDropUpload.ts) near dragDrop/dragDropCore; update imports/tests so drag-drop utility ownership is consistently in shared/lib.

findings.summary: HuggingFace-specific API-key orchestration sits in a broad shared hooks package.
evidence: The exported surface is useHuggingFaceToken, and implementation is directly coupled to externalApiKeys repository and HuggingFace token verification behavior.
Despite service-specific scope, the file is organized under src/shared/hooks (global hook bucket), which weakens domain ownership boundaries for integration code.
findings.suggestion: Relocate this hook to an integration/service-owned package (for example src/shared/services/externalApiKeys/hooks/useHuggingFaceToken.ts or an integrations/huggingface area), keep shared/hooks for cross-domain generic hooks only, and move/retarget its test alongside the new owner package.

findings.summary: Large orchestration hooks mix too many concerns into single control surfaces.
evidence: `useTimelineOrchestrator` centralizes multiple orthogonal behaviors (zoom math, drag/drop mechanics, selection model, endpoint drag, browser-driven video insertion, pending frame reconciliation) in one 680-line hook.

`useImageEditOrchestrator` composes inpainting/magic-edit/reposition/img2img and then builds a very broad context payload with dozens of fields and handlers, including placeholder values for not-yet-wired state.
`useTimelineCore` mixes persistence orchestration, data derivation, metadata manipulation, RPC lifecycle management, and logging in a single hook API.
findings.suggestion: Split each orchestrator into smaller domain hooks with explicit ownership boundaries (interaction, persistence, derived view-models). Keep one thin composition hook per feature that only wires sub-hooks and exports a minimal public contract.

findings.summary: Shot editor controllers rely on oversized parameter/return contracts that increase coupling.
evidence: `UseGenerationControllerParams` contains a very long flat list of mostly related settings and callbacks, then forwards many unchanged into `useGenerateBatch`.
`UseEditingControllerParams` similarly aggregates many unrelated domains (structure video, audio, naming, join settings) and returns a broad merged surface.
`ShotEditorLayoutProps` mirrors this breadth with an extensive prop list spanning header, timeline, generation, modals, and section-level behavior.
findings.suggestion: Introduce typed sub-models (`generationSettings`, `joinSettings`, `mediaSettings`, `uiActions`) and pass those objects across boundaries. Move shared state into feature-scoped context or dedicated controller objects to reduce prop drilling and callsite fragility.

findings.summary: Pane components combine shell UI, domain workflows, and side-effect orchestration.
evidence: `GenerationsPane` handles pane mechanics, filtering UI, modal lifecycle, gallery data coordination, event listeners, and shot-creation side effects in one component.
`TasksPane` handles pane shell behavior plus task query orchestration, cancellation flows, sessionStorage policy, lightbox integration, and project-scope filtering in one component.
Both panes duplicate structural patterns for lock/open state orchestration and feature-specific workflow logic inside top-level UI components.
findings.suggestion: Refactor pane modules into: (1) presentational pane shell components, (2) headless state hooks for filters/pagination, and (3) domain action hooks for mutations/lightbox/task flows. Reuse a shared pane-state adapter to avoid repeated pane-control scaffolding.

findings.summary: Supabase edge functions use divergent runtime scaffolding patterns within one subsystem.
evidence: `claim-next-task` uses shared primitives (`initEdgeRuntime`, `ensureRequestMethod`, `parseJsonBodyLoose`) for consistent request lifecycle handling.
`generate-upload-url` and `calculate-task-cost` manually repeat env checks, client creation, method/body validation, and response shaping instead of the shared runtime path.
This creates structural inconsistency and duplicated operational logic (auth/error/JSON/CORS handling) across function endpoints.
findings.suggestion: Standardize edge functions on one shared runtime entry pattern (`_shared/edgeRequest` or `_shared/edgeOperation`) and migrate legacy functions incrementally so auth/method/body/error contracts are centralized.

findings.summary: Core task payload contracts rely on permissive Record<string, unknown> objects and cast-based parsing instead of explicit typed schemas.
evidence: joinClips payload builders and mode helpers return Record<string, unknown> throughout orchestration assembly (src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts:345,368,419,464,482,500,510,528).
imageGeneration also returns unstructured payload records for task params (src/shared/lib/tasks/imageGeneration.ts:327,348,426).

Downstream consumers re-interpret these records using cast-and-probe logic, e.g. `item.shot_data as Record<string, unknown>` and `(variant.params ?? {}) as Record<string, unknown>` (src/shared/lib/generationTransformers.ts:335,456).
findings.suggestion: Define task-family-specific runtime schemas (e.g., zod/io-ts) and inferred TS types for orchestrator details/contracts, then replace Record<string, unknown> return types with validated domain types end-to-end.

findings.summary: Several high-traffic modules are oversized and mix unrelated responsibilities, making navigation and safe change-scoping difficult.
evidence: taskParamsUtils is 850 lines and handles parsing, contract selection, prompt/image derivation, and lora extraction in one file.

ImageLightbox is 766 lines with environment bootstrapping, shared state orchestration, and rendering concerns in one component module (src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx:100-223 and beyond).
JoinClipsPage includes multiple local hooks plus page rendering and DnD orchestration in one file (src/tools/join-clips/pages/JoinClipsPage.tsx:47-170, 201+).
findings.suggestion: Split by responsibility boundaries: isolate pure derivation/parsing helpers, extract page-level orchestration hooks into dedicated files, and leave component files focused on rendering and composition.

findings.summary: Frequent generic naming in transformation/orchestration paths reduces intent clarity and makes maintenance slower.
evidence: Repeated broad identifiers (`params`, `base`, `payload`, `details`) are used for semantically different structures in the same function scope (src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts:345-469).

taskParams utilities repeatedly alias `parsedParams` to `params`, then branch across unrelated task families, obscuring which schema is expected at each callsite (src/shared/lib/taskParamsUtils.ts:63-123,138-220).

generationTransformers uses similarly broad names (`item`, `options`, `baseItem`) in dense transformation logic, reducing signal in a central mapping path (src/shared/lib/generationTransformers.ts:320-380).
findings.suggestion: Adopt schema-specific naming conventions (e.g., `orchestratorDetails`, `segmentTaskParams`, `timelinePayload`, `variantParams`) and enforce via lint rules/review checklist for core transformation modules.

findings.summary: Two near-identical result/failure primitives use different naming vocabularies, creating avoidable API discovery friction.
evidence: Both modules define almost equivalent success/failure unions with policy/recoverable/errorCode/message/cause, but with different type and function names (`Operation*` vs `Edge*`).
The divergence is semantic rather than behavioral, so developers must memorize duplicate conventions for client/runtime and edge runtime code paths.
findings.suggestion: Consolidate on one shared naming contract (or documented mapping) for result primitives across app and edge layers to reduce cognitive overhead and onboarding ambiguity.
